title: bromus condensatus subsp. condensatus
intro: Bromus condensatus subsp. condensatus, also known as the clustered bromegrass or bunch grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Bromus condensatus subsp. condensatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from pain and itching. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including skin irritations and minor wounds.
